title: DSDS Pattern Definitions
description: Defines documents for interaction patterns. Patterns are repeated solutions to common UX problems: navigation, error messaging, form validation, empty states, loading sequences, and onboarding flows. A pattern document holds the identity of a multi-component solution. The `metadata` object carries optional metadata fields: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. Typed entries in the `documentBlock` array carry structured docs.

pattern.description: A repeated, multi-component solution to a common UX problem. Patterns sit above individual components and show how those components compose to meet a user need. The top level holds identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` object holds optional metadata fields — status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. The `documentBlock` array holds structured docs as typed entries: `interactions` for flow steps, `guidelines` for rules, `accessibility` for a11y specs, and so on.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a pattern.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the pattern (e.g., 'error-messaging', 'navigation', 'empty-state', 'form-validation'). MUST be unique within its entity group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Error Messaging', 'Navigation', 'Empty State').

description.description: What this pattern is, the UX problem it solves, and how its components compose to meet a user need. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this pattern, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Patterns accept the pattern-specific kinds (interactions) plus the general kinds (guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content, sections). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Interaction entries rely on order to show the flow in time. Reference the components a pattern uses through the `links` metadata field (kind 'component', name, and role) rather than a dedicated block kind.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured documentation for agent (AI/LLM) consumption only — a partitioned space accepting the same document block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Use it for guidance that would be noise for human readers: generation constraints, disambiguation between confusable entities, machine-checkable rules with evidence. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing documentation. Agents SHOULD read both arrays — human-facing blocks first, then these.
